zone_6b_notes: Hardy in Zone 6b but requires very specific soil — acidic, pH 4.5–5.5, well-drained, rich in organic matter. Missouri soils are typically neutral to slightly acidic; pH amendment with sulfur is almost always required and must be done months before planting. Plant at least two different varieties for cross-pollination and better yields. Mulch heavily with pine bark or wood chips (4–6 inches) to maintain acidity and moisture. Blueberries are slow to establish — minimal harvest years 1–2, full production at year 5–7. Bluecrop and Blueray are the most reliable highbush varieties in Zone 6b Missouri.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant bare-root or potted stock in spring (April) after soil pH amendment
